entry.title: Desk Photos 2024
entry.content: Repurposed my laundry room table as my desk after selling everything I own. You can see my travel NAS on top of my UPS in the corner on the floor.
entry.summary: Repurposed my laundry room table as my desk after selling everything I own. You can see my travel NAS on top of my UPS in the corner on the floor.

entry.title: Travel NAS 2024
entry.content: Last year I made up my mind to move away from Austin and sell all of my belongings, though I swear I’m not a minimalist. This included my homelab. However I still wanted to keep my data with me and ruled out cloud storage or a simple portable hard drive. I wanted a portable NAS for resilliency and I’ve grown to like TrueNAS.
entry.summary: Last year I made up my mind to move away from Austin and sell all of my belongings, though I swear I’m not a minimalist. This included my homelab. However I still wanted to keep my data with me and ruled out cloud storage or a simple portable hard drive. I wanted a portable NAS for resilliency and I’ve grown to like TrueNAS.

entry.title: Desk Photos 2023
entry.content: Temporary desk at my new job.
entry.summary: Temporary desk at my new job.

entry.title: Desk Photo 2022
entry.content: After moving back to Austin and settling in my big apartment.
entry.summary: After moving back to Austin and settling in my big apartment.

entry.title: Custom Fabricated Rack 2022
entry.content: I got a couple of items that warranted rewiring my TV cabinet rack: StarTech extenders and P-Link server rails. I inverted the extenders so I could recess the patch panel and switch, making room for the SFP modules and fiber cables. I can’t believe I can’t find any right angle adapters for the LC/UPC fiber patch cables!
entry.summary: I got a couple of items that warranted rewiring my TV cabinet rack: StarTech extenders and P-Link server rails. I inverted the extenders so I could recess the patch panel and switch, making room for the SFP modules and fiber cables. I can’t believe I can’t find any right angle adapters for the LC/UPC fiber patch cables!

entry.title: Custom Fabricated Rack 2020
entry.content: Having my equipment hidden in a TV cabinet was all fine and well but after a while I got the server rack itch. So during the pandemic I learned Fusion 360 and made my own custom 10u rack posts. It turned out nicer than I expected! 3D models are available here: Robpol86/TV-Cabinet-Rack-Posts
entry.summary: Having my equipment hidden in a TV cabinet was all fine and well but after a while I got the server rack itch. So during the pandemic I learned Fusion 360 and made my own custom 10u rack posts. It turned out nicer than I expected! 3D models are available here: https://github.com/Robpol86/TV-Cabinet-Rack-Posts

entry.title: Desk Photos 2019
entry.content: Changed teams and finally got a corner cubicle.
entry.summary: Changed teams and finally got a corner cubicle.

entry.title: Desk Photo 2018
entry.content: New apartment to cut down on my commute and new computer to take advantage of my 10 GbE home network and server. Upgraded from a Mac Pro to the new iMac Pro! 10 core Xeon + 4 TiB SSD + 128 GiB RAM - $3,000 employee discount = still a lot of money.
entry.summary: New apartment to cut down on my commute and new computer to take advantage of my 10 GbE home network and server. Upgraded from a Mac Pro to the new iMac Pro! 10 core Xeon + 4 TiB SSD + 128 GiB RAM - $3,000 employee discount = still a lot of money.

entry.title: TV Cabinet Rack 2017
entry.content: I replaced my super cool Dell rack recently with a more normal-looking setup in my living room. After countless weeks looking for the right TV cabinet I finally found one deep enough to house a couple of 2U rackmount cases and a rackmount switch. It turned out nicer than I had expected! Don’t ask me how much all this cost.
entry.summary: I replaced my super cool Dell rack recently with a more normal-looking setup in my living room. After countless weeks looking for the right TV cabinet I finally found one deep enough to house a couple of 2U rackmount cases and a rackmount switch. It turned out nicer than I had expected! Don’t ask me how much all this cost.

entry.title: Desk Photos 2017
entry.content: I’ve decided to get rid of my rack cabinet in my living room, so I started by moving my Mac Pro onto my desk and running ethernet cables through the wall. Also with my larger IKEA bed I had to rotate my desk 90 degrees to make everything fit.
entry.summary: I’ve decided to get rid of my rack cabinet in my living room, so I started by moving my Mac Pro onto my desk and running ethernet cables through the wall. Also with my larger IKEA bed I had to rotate my desk 90 degrees to make everything fit.

entry.title: Dell Rack 2016
entry.content: My server rack cabinet at home. Built my own router and zip tied a keystone panel so pulling out my rack doesn’t involve disconnecting my speaker wire. Listen to https://youtu.be/WPDZoOKX8ag to hear how much noise this puts out (not really that much). For pix of the underside look at last year’s gallery.
entry.summary: My server rack cabinet at home. Built my own router and zip tied a keystone panel so pulling out my rack doesn’t involve disconnecting my speaker wire. Listen to https://youtu.be/WPDZoOKX8ag to hear how much noise this puts out (not really that much). For pix of the underside look at last year’s gallery.

entry.title: Desk Photos 2016
entry.content: Right before I moved desks for the 6th time in 1.5 years. I lost my nice window desk, oh well.
entry.summary: Right before I moved desks for the 6th time in 1.5 years. I lost my nice window desk, oh well.

entry.title: Desk Photo 2015: Work @ Uber
entry.content: I really like standing desks and luckily Uber provides this contraption. Everyone seems to have one.
entry.summary: I really like standing desks and luckily Uber provides this contraption. Everyone seems to have one.

entry.title: Dell Rack 2015
entry.content: My server rack cabinet at home. Removed everything from it, cleaned and dusted it, and installed only the things I’m using.
entry.summary: My server rack cabinet at home. Removed everything from it, cleaned and dusted it, and installed only the things I’m using.

entry.title: Desk Photos 2014
entry.content: Got back to having a standing desk. I stole this rolling desk since they moved us to a really cramped area and damned if I’m giving up one of my monitors.
entry.summary: Got back to having a standing desk. I stole this rolling desk since they moved us to a really cramped area and damned if I’m giving up one of my monitors.

entry.title: Dell Rack 2013
entry.content: Taken after finally re-wiring it and setting it up after moving to San Francisco. I only took one photo of it :(
entry.summary: Taken after finally re-wiring it and setting it up after moving to San Francisco. I only took one photo of it :(

entry.title: Desk Photos 2013
entry.content: I took this picture shortly before leaving Symantec. The office kept shrinking, people leaving but nobody being hired. I managed to get a nice window cubicle for a few months in the end.
entry.summary: I took this picture shortly before leaving Symantec. The office kept shrinking, people leaving but nobody being hired. I managed to get a nice window cubicle for a few months in the end.

entry.title: Dell Rack 2012
entry.content: After over a whole year since receiving it I have finally made full use of my rack and transferred all of my computers and equipment to it.
entry.summary: After over a whole year since receiving it I have finally made full use of my rack and transferred all of my computers and equipment to it.

entry.title: Desk Photos 2012
entry.content: I shamefully upgraded from my custom built dual core AMD FX-60 computer to a quad core Intel Core2 Dell computer I borrowed from work. I’ve had that FX-60 for too long and it was showing its age. Also that KVM was so horrible.
entry.summary: I shamefully upgraded from my custom built dual core AMD FX-60 computer to a quad core Intel Core2 Dell computer I borrowed from work. I’ve had that FX-60 for too long and it was showing its age. Also that KVM was so horrible.

entry.title: Dell Rack 2011
entry.content: Ever since I took a Cisco CCNA class back in high school in 2002 I’ve always wanted a rack at home. I even bought one on Craigslist in 2007 for $100 but it had round holes and it was very shallow. Turns out it was meant for music equipment.
entry.summary: Ever since I took a Cisco CCNA class back in high school in 2002 I’ve always wanted a rack at home. I even bought one on Craigslist in 2007 for $100 but it had round holes and it was very shallow. Turns out it was meant for music equipment.

entry.title: Desk Photos 2011
entry.content: The same cubicle shortly before another office downsize.
entry.summary: The same cubicle shortly before another office downsize.

entry.title: Desk Photo 2010: Work @ Symantec
entry.content: After the office consolidated into one floor (instead of two), this was my new cubicle.
entry.summary: After the office consolidated into one floor (instead of two), this was my new cubicle.

entry.title: Desk Photos 2009
entry.content: After moving buildings we didn’t have offices anymore, we had cubicles. These are actually really nice cubes!
entry.summary: After moving buildings we didn’t have offices anymore, we had cubicles. These are actually really nice cubes!

entry.title: Desk Photos 2007
entry.content: I shared this office with my coworker while I was a contractor at Symantec.
entry.summary: I shared this office with my coworker while I was a contractor at Symantec.

entry.title: Desk Photo 2006
entry.content: First year in Austin after my computer died. I was stuck using that laptop for months.
entry.summary: First year in Austin after my computer died. I was stuck using that laptop for months.

entry.title: Desk Photo 2005: Work @ TISD
entry.content: This was right before I left my first job for Austin. I loved that Dell Latitude C400 laptop (my first laptop) that I bought on eBay. I think it was a Pentium 3 866 MHz. And that’s a brownie.
entry.summary: This was right before I left my first job for Austin. I loved that Dell Latitude C400 laptop (my first laptop) that I bought on eBay. I think it was a Pentium 3 866 MHz. And that’s a brownie.

entry.title: Desk Photo 2004
entry.content: Got my kickass APC SUA1500 UPS! Weeks after getting it the house had a serious electrical problem and caused a brownout on my computer’s circuit. It would have been destroyed if it weren’t for my UPS. I also dabbled in the world of iptables and IP masquerading with my Linux router on a floppy.
entry.summary: Got my kickass APC SUA1500 UPS! Weeks after getting it the house had a serious electrical problem and caused a brownout on my computer’s circuit. It would have been destroyed if it weren’t for my UPS. I also dabbled in the world of iptables and IP masquerading with my Linux router on a floppy.

entry.title: Desk Photos 2003
entry.content: Started by a thread called “Where You Rock” on Big-O-Software Forums in early 2003, I began to take pictures of my desk to show off. Since then I have tried to take pictures of all my desks after significant changes for nostalgic purposes.
entry.summary: Started by a thread called “Where You Rock” on Big-O-Software Forums in early 2003, I began to take pictures of my desk to show off. Since then I have tried to take pictures of all my desks after significant changes for nostalgic purposes.
